decisions.summary: Implemented Claude channel permission relay at the bridge boundary instead of inventing a parallel dashboard-only permission path, so operator decisions travel over the same MCP notification protocol that Claude Code already understands.

Modeled pending permission prompts as first-class domain events and read-model state (`agent.permission_requested` and `agent.permission_resolved`) so the dashboard snapshot, event stream, and activity log stay consistent from one source of truth.

Used only the protocol-supported decision verbs `allow` and `deny`. The issue wording mentions richer choices, but Claude's current channel permission protocol exposes only those two behaviors, so the implementation must match protocol reality rather than invent unsupported semantics.

Drove the operator UI directly from event-sourced `channelPermissionRequestsById` state in the shared dashboard store, so the modal opens from the same snapshot and event stream data that the activity log and other panels consume instead of adding another polling channel.

Finished the feature with focused regression coverage on the real permission flow edges that can drift silently: request ordering in the selector, App-level allow submission, and App-level deny submission.

hazards.summary: The MCP SDK path for custom notification registration is awkward here and the bridge currently installs the permission-request handler by reaching into the server notification-handlers map.
hazards.mitigation: Keep the payload parser strict, cover the behavior with bridge tests, and do not widen the handler to unrelated notifications without first replacing this hook with an upstream-supported API.

hazards.summary: The bridge/backend plumbing is done, but dashboard UI work is still separate. Backend snapshots and events now carry pending permission requests, yet operators still need the modal bead to make the flow usable end to end.
hazards.mitigation: After this bead passes inspection, implement workspace-ksbv by wiring the frontend store and permission modal directly to the new pending-request state and response endpoint.

hazards.summary: Manual `pan inspect` is currently broken in this workspace because it looks for `dist/cli/prompts/inspect-agent.md`, but the built prompt that exists is `dist/prompts/inspect-agent.md`.
hazards.mitigation: Do not trust manual inspect requests until that prompt-path bug is fixed. If inspection becomes mandatory for progress, fix the command path resolution instead of bypassing the specialist flow.

resumePoint.description: Workspace-kmfs is complete and locally verified. Next step: stage the final focused tests plus continue-state update, create one commit for workspace-kmfs, close the bead, then run the full repository quality gates before pushing and calling `pan done`.

sessionHistory.note: Rebuilt the missing PAN-987 continue state, verified the claimed workspace-g86b bead remains scoped to bridge/backend permission relay plumbing, and confirmed local typecheck plus targeted tests were already green before commit/close.

Committed and closed workspace-g86b, then implemented workspace-ksbv by adding a dashboard permission modal, a selector for pending channel permission requests, App-level response handling to `/api/agents/:id/permissions/:requestId/respond`, and focused frontend tests covering rendering plus allow action submission.

Finished workspace-kmfs by adding final regression coverage for deny submission and selector ordering, then re-ran the combined permission relay test suite across frontend, bridge, delivery, and contract reducers with typecheck green.
